pi_items.source: Verified in task.json metadata, setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and verifier.py. Full path: /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/pipe_elbow.FCStd
Calculated from task specifications: OD=25mm (dimension 1), centerline_radius + outer_radius = 40 + 12.5 = 52.5mm (dimensions 2 & 3). Dimensions sorted as [25.0, 52.5, 52.5].
Tolerance of ±8.0mm per dimension is reasonable for hand-modeled CAD geometry, allowing ±15% variation on larger dimensions and ±32% on smaller dimension.

Calculated ideal volume for 90-degree hollow pipe elbow: cross_section_area = π*(12.5² - 10.5²) = 144.51 mm², centerline_length = (π/2)*40 = 62.83 mm, volume = 9080.04 mm³. Min threshold of 5500 mm³ is appropriate.
Calculated ideal volume: 9080.04 mm³. Max threshold of 14000 mm³ allows reasonable variation for approximation errors. Ratio calculated/max = 0.649, provides ~54% margin.
A properly modeled hollow pipe elbow is expected to have at least 4 faces: top annular face, bottom annular face, outer curved surface, inner curved surface.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic CAD modeling task with no external data sources. The pipe elbow specifications are: OD=25mm (radius 12.5mm), ID=21mm (radius 10.5mm, wall thickness 2mm), centerline bend radius=40mm, 90-degree angle. All metadata claims have been verified: expected output filename is 'pipe_elbow.FCStd', target sorted bounding box is [25.0, 52.5, 52.5] with tolerance ±8.0mm, expected volume is ~9080 mm³ (range 5500-14000 mm³), and minimum 4 faces required for valid hollow solid.
